headline: Run zone 1, tip canes — Friday storms incoming.
weather_notes: Run zone 1 this morning — 4 days dry at 98°F is past the 1-day re-water threshold; afternoon's 30% storm chance isn't reliable enough to skip.
Do all outdoor work before 8 AM today through Saturday — 96–98°F daily highs close every outdoor window by mid-morning.
Apply fish emulsion before 9 AM today — Friday's 46% and Saturday's 41% afternoon storm chances will wash off same-day foliar applications before they absorb.
Harvest blackberries before noon — today's afternoon storm chance will bruise ripe Triple Crown fruit on the cane and accelerate spoilage.

surfaced_tasks.title: Tip new blackberry primocanes if 3–4 ft tall
surfaced_tasks.reason: 6 days overdue; today's early morning is the best outdoor window this week before Friday–Saturday storms and continued 96–98°F heat.

suggested_tasks.title: Feed e1–e4 tomatoes with fish emulsion
suggested_tasks.description: 26-day transplants with no logged fertilizer; apply this morning before Friday's 46% storm chance washes off the application.

suggested_tasks.title: Feed w1 strawberries with berry food
suggested_tasks.description: 43 days since the last June 3 feed; apply Saturday morning before afternoon storms so the feed has time to absorb.
